title: Understanding the Fear of God
reading:
Many people misunderstand the fear of God. Scripture reveals that the fear of God is not simply being afraid of punishment. Rather, it is a holy reverence that acknowledges God's majesty, power, holiness, authority, and love.

The Hebrew word often translated "fear" includes awe, respect, reverence, and worship. When we fear God properly, we place Him above all else in our lives.

The fear of God is the foundation of spiritual wisdom because it establishes the proper relationship between Creator and creation. Without fear of God, people become self-governing. With fear of God, they become God-governed.
reflection:
What comes to mind when you hear "fear of God"?

Do you view God primarily as holy or familiar?

How might greater reverence transform your walk with Him?
abide: Lord, teach me to see You as You truly are. Replace casual attitudes with holy reverence and awe.
